According to Prahldda the liiies on the body constitute the
Mudrds imprinted by Isvara. Hence the body becomes Samudra or
Samudraka. So this science is termed Sdmudrika. This Chapter (LXVIII)
has many ideas that are common to the Vdlmiki Rdmdyana. This has
been shown in appropriate places in the notes. Haldyudha must have
written a work on Sdmudrika fas trd. For, in his commentary on the
Pingala Chandattdstra there are given a large number of illustrative
verses bearing on this interesting subject. Verses 89 and 116 are
excellent both as poetry and as portraits of truth. The latter giving
four constituents of real happiness, viz., food, drink, conveyance and
